title: Bomarea ovallei
intro: Bomarea ovallei, also known as Oval Leaf Bomarea, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Alstroemeriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Bomarea ovallei include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal infusions and decoctions, as well as tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Bomarea ovallei is a species of flowering plant in the family Alstroemeriaceae. It is native to Peru. This plant belongs to the genus Bomarea and is part of the Alstroemeriaceae family. Within the plant kingdom, it is classified as a monocotyledon, having one cotyledon in its seeds. Further classification also includes its placement in the order Asparagales.
